rulings.Case Title: SUSAN HULL BOSTWICK VS. FRANCHISE TAX BOARD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Compel Disclosure Of The Money Of Account, Medium Exchange And The Attorney Licenses Of All Of The Attorneys Who Worked On This Case And A Billing Breakdown
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 19, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER SUSAN BOSTWICK's Motion To Compel Disclosure Of The Money Of Account, Medium Exchange And The Attorney Licenses Of All Of The Attorneys Who Worked On This Case And A Billing Breakdown. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Judgment was entered in this case on July 17, 2019. There has been no petition for attorneys' fees nor memorandum of costs filed by the prevailing party. Accordingly, there is no basis for this motion and it is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION VS. BODUM USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant R.J. Thomas Manufacturing Company, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 19, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant R.J. Thomas Manufacturing Company, Inc. Plaintiff Ecological Rights Foundation's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and for entry of consent judgment (R.J. Thomas Manufacturing Company, Inc.) is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The court will sign the proposed order and consent judgment included with the moving papers. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD P. VAN SIEGMAN VS. FCA US, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Further Hearing On Plaintiff Ronald Van Siegman'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Qualified/Person Most Knowledgeable (Specially Set At 1:30 Pm Before Judge Pro Tem Adrienne Rogers)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 19, 2019, Line 2, Further Hearing On Plaintiff Ronald Van Siegman'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Qualified/Person Most Knowledgeable (Specially Set At 1:30 Pm Before Judge Pro Tem Adrienne Rogers).(part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Adrienne Rogers, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This matter is specially set at 1:30 p.m. before Judge Pro Tem Adrienne Rogers. In June 2019, plaintiff moved to compel the deposition of defendant's PMQ on 39 categories of information. On July 25, the court ordered the parties to meet and confer and reserved jurisdiction on the merits. The parties met and conferred; during that process, plaintiff withdrew numerous categories and revised the definition of several terms, including "Engine Defects" and "Electrical Defects." On August 13, the parties submitted a joint separate statement on the remaining contested categories: 7, 8, 12, 13, 15, 16, 20-25, 28, and 29. Having considered the moving and opposition papers, and the joint separate statement, the court rules follows: Defendant must produce a person most qualified to testify on its behalf (Code Civ. Proc., § 2025.230) on the following categories of information listed in plaintiff's April 2019 second amended deposition notice, subject to the revised definitions of Engine Defects and Electrical Defects as set forth in the parties' joint separate statement: Categories 7, 8, 15, and 16: GRANTED. Defendant's boilerplate objections are overruled. Categories 12 and 20: DENIED. These categories are overbroad. Category 13: GRANTED to the extent it seeks information regarding available fixes for Engine Defects in vehicles of the same model, make, and year as the vehicle at issue. Category 21: GRANTED to the extent it seeks information regarding available fixes for Electrical Defects in vehicles of the same model, make, and year as the vehicle at issue. Category 22: GRANTED. Defendant's boilerplate objections are overruled. Category 23, 24, 25: GRANTED as to the year 2018, when plaintiff allegedly demanded repurchase of the vehicle. Categories 28 and 29: GRANTED. Defendant's boilerplate objections are overruled. No monetary sanctions. (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LUU TRAN ET AL VS. JAVELIN LOGISTICS COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, August 19, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT JAVELIN LOGISTICS COMPANY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Javelin Logistics Company, Inc.'s demurrer to the first amended complaint is overruled. The demurrer is overruled as to causes of action one through three as plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to impose successor liability on movant. Plaintiff alleges facts showing that movant was a mere continuation of the prior entity. (First Amended Complaint, Paras. 31-34; McClellan v. Northridge Park Townhome Owners Ass'n, Inc. (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 746, 753 [successor corporation can be liable where "the transaction amounts to a consolidation or merger of the two corporations" or "the purchasing corporation is merely a continuation of the selling corporation."].) The demurrer is overruled as to cause of action seven. Even if a fraudulent transfer cause of action generally has a heightened pleading standard because it implicates "fraud," that standard is relaxed where "the defendant may be assumed to have knowledge of the facts equal to that possessed by the plaintiff." (Ludgate Ins. Co., Ltd. v. Lockheed Martin Corp. (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 592, 608; see also Daniels v. Select Portfolio Servicing, Inc. (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1150, 1167.) In this case, movant is in the best positon to know the details of the asset purchase agreement and plaintiff alleges sufficient ultimate facts to support the claim. Plaintiff alleges that the transaction was not supported by adequate consideration and the court takes that allegation as true on demurrer. (First Amended Complaint, Paras. 39, 93.) Movant's authorities are distinguishable. (See Lo v. Lee (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 1065, 1072-1075 [order sustaining demurrer affirmed where tuition payments made to a university by an insolvent debtor parent cannot be recovered from the debtor's child, who was not the beneficiary of the transfer]; Fox v. MGM Grand Hotels, Inc. (1982) 137 Cal.App.3d 524, 527 [trial court properly sustained demurrer to fraudulent transfer claim where allegations in the complaint disclosed the transfer of consideration]; Kuhlman v. Pac. States Sav. & Loan Co. (1941) 17 Cal.2d 820, 821-822 [affirming order sustaining demurrer to fraudulent transfer claim where there was no allegation that the consideration was inadequate].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name, and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Deem The Request For Admission To Cross-Complainant William Mora Admitted And Compel Answers And Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 19, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF BRYANT FU's Motion For Order To Deem The Request For Admission To Cross-Complainant William Mora Admitted And Compel Answers And Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Based on the papers filed in this case, it is impossible for the Court to discern which party is being truthful and which party is not. For that reason, the motion is DENIED. However, the Court deems the discovery requests attached as Exhibit A to Cross-Defendant Fu's declaration (i.e., Requests for Admission Nos. 1-3, Special Interrogatories Nos. 1-3, and Requests for Production of Documents Nos. 1-5) served on Cross-Complainant Mora as of August 19, 2019. Mora to serve verified responses within 30 days of that date. In the interest of judicial economy and promoting candor with the Court, the Court further Orders as follows: For the remainder of this case, all papers for which proof of service is required, such service must be accomplished through a means that provides for tracking of the delivery. Parties are Ordered to provide each other with an address for service that has the capacity to receive such deliveries. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
